title: Build and Test an MCP Server with Official SDKs · Retrieval Circuit
summary: Implement a typed server with tools, resources, and automated protocol tests. Learners retrieve rules and procedures before seeing feedback. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for a runnable tested MCP server after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Select an official maintained SDK and pin a compatible protocol target.
Create server metadata, capabilities, handlers, and transport entry points.
Validate structured inputs and return typed content or structured errors.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""Local MCP protocol and tool-contract portfolio."""

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: Any abandoned wrapper is equivalent to an official SDK. This fails the Sdk Selection contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

steps.title: Separate evidence from confidence
steps.instruction:
Use a changed review case derived from this module: agent ext server build. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.

Inspect the Inspector Debug trace for this review case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for use protocol inspection and client logs to diagnose negotiation and transport failures.

content.text: For Inspector Debug, distinguish a claim from evidence by locating the relevant precondition, trace or invariant, and changed input result.

stimulus.title: Inspector Debug case record
stimulus.caption: Three excerpts from a learner evidence packet; only one connects a claim to a reproducible check.

lines.label: assumption
lines.text: Use a changed review case derived from this module: agent ext server build. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.

lines.label: risk
lines.text: A connection failure always means authentication is wrong.

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use protocol inspection and client logs to diagnose negotiation and transport failures. Artifact under review: a runnable tested MCP server.

options.text: The record names the assumption, applies Inspector Debug, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.
The record says the result looks right but supplies no quantity, trace, condition, or counterexample.
Apply the visible technique immediately, omit assumptions, and treat one successful output as complete proof.

steps.title: Record what required effort
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Inspector Debug applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Use protocol inspection and client logs to diagnose negotiation and transport failures.

hints: Name the Inspector Debug precondition or invariant that the review case must preserve.
Trace this concrete case before choosing: Use a changed review case derived from this module: agent ext server build. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.
Use the documented misconception as a counterexample target: A connection failure always means authentication is wrong.
